task_summary:
**Task:** A fragile blender arrived damaged in transit, and the correct resolution has two distinct pieces: the return refund and a separate $10 fragile-item goodwill credit. The right handling is to preserve those as separate actions rather than burying everything inside one blended refund.

**Challenge:** Action boundaries are what matter here. Weak agents forget the goodwill credit altogether or merge it into the item refund in a way that loses the actual structure of the remedy.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must process return for full refund of $89.
Agent must issue $10 fragile-item goodwill credit via separate process_refund call.
Agent must not combine the goodwill into the return refund.
Agent must include the $10 fragile goodwill credit entirely.
Agent must not add $10 to the return refund instead of issuing via separate process_refund.
Agent must include the $10 fragile goodwill credit in the final resolution.
Agent must not combine the goodwill credit into the item return refund.

opening_message: My blender from order ORD-7003 arrived completely smashed — the box was torn up and the glass pitcher is broken. I need this resolved.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: Your blender arrived badly damaged, and you want the issue fully resolved rather than reduced to a bare-minimum refund. The condition of the package makes the shipping failure feel especially obvious to you. You expect the company to take responsibility in a way that reflects both the damaged item and the bad delivery experience.
known_info: issue: arrived damaged, glass pitcher shattered
task_rules: Give the order ID when asked.
Describe the damage clearly: 'The glass pitcher is shattered.'
If the agent mentions a goodwill credit in addition to the refund, say you appreciate that.
If the agent only mentions the refund and not the goodwill, ask 'Is there any additional compensation for the inconvenience? This was a fragile item.'
